pricing.note: Embedding happens once at ingest; runtime inference is arithmetic on cached vectors.

verticals.label: Telco
verticals.description: Subscriber upgrade propensity vs churn — marketing activation

eval.methodology: Illustrative offline evaluation on 62 simulated held-out sessions with authored ground-truth labels. Included to show the shape of a model health ledger, not as a real benchmark.

anchors.phrase: comparing unlimited premium plan tiers and pricing
checking device upgrade eligibility in the account app
viewing 5G unlimited plan pricing page
estimating the monthly cost of upgrading my plan
browsing new phone deals and trade-in offers
adding a premium data add-on to the cart
checking trade-in value for my current phone
reading about unlimited plan benefits and perks
reviewing a plan upgrade order at checkout
long focused session inside the carrier account app
opening the mobile app every day this week
running a network speed test from the app
exploring the 5G coverage map in detail
reading support documentation thoroughly
reviewing data usage on the account dashboard
configuring account settings and preferences
heavy mobile data usage streaming video
contacting support asking how to cancel service
complaining about poor network quality and dropped calls
monthly bill payment failed and is overdue
comparing competitor carrier prices and offers
requesting the account number needed to port out
disputing unexpected charges on the bill
downgrading to a cheaper plan tier
reading how to cancel my contract without fees

scenarios.label: Baseline session
scenarios.description: A fresh, coherent session: on-device plan research plus a cloud billing overage. Signals agree — high-confidence upgrade propensity.

payload.screen: plan upgrade comparison — unlimited premium 5G tiers

events.serialized: mobile app event — app screen dwell. screen: plan upgrade comparison — unlimited premium 5G tiers; compared: premium unlimited vs current 20GB plan; dwell seconds: 340

payload.type: data cap overage — monthly allowance exceeded

events.serialized: cloud event — billing event. type: data cap overage — monthly allowance exceeded; overage gb: 4.2; recommended plan: unlimited 5G upgrade

payload.follow_up: checked 5G coverage before choosing plan upgrade

events.serialized: mobile app event — network probe. action: ran network speed test; result mbps: 48; follow up: checked 5G coverage before choosing plan upgrade

payload.action: checked trade-in value for current phone

events.serialized: mobile app event — app screen dwell. screen: device upgrade eligibility checker; action: checked trade-in value for current phone

payload.referrer: google search — best unlimited 5g plan upgrade

events.serialized: cloud event — web visit. url: /plans/unlimited-premium-benefits; referrer: google search — best unlimited 5g plan upgrade; pages in session: 3

payload.topic: asked about cancellation fees when leaving

events.serialized: cloud event — support ticket. topic: asked about cancellation fees when leaving; channel: chat; resolved: closed — no action taken

scenarios.label: Asynchronous conflict
scenarios.description: Fresh on-device signals scream upgrade; stale cloud signals say churn (a cancel enquiry and a failed payment from last week). Time decay resolves the tie — and honestly lowers confidence.

events.serialized: mobile app event — app screen dwell. screen: unlimited premium plan pricing; dwell seconds: 420; local velocity: high
cloud event — support ticket. topic: asked how to cancel service; sentiment: negative; channel: chat

payload.action: added premium data add-on to cart

events.serialized: mobile app event — cart event. action: added premium data add-on to cart; value usd: 25
cloud event — billing event. type: payment failed; amount usd: 68.5; retry: pending
mobile app event — app screen dwell. screen: checkout — plan upgrade review order; step: review order
cloud event — web visit. url: /support/how-to-cancel; referrer: direct

scenarios.label: Sparse / drifting signals
scenarios.description: Weak, stale, contradictory signals. The honest answer is "we don't know" — confidence falls below the 70% floor and the system refuses to emit a segment. Guardrails over guesses.

events.serialized: mobile app event — app open. screen: home dashboard; dwell seconds: 12
cloud event — web visit. url: /support/articles/roaming-charges; referrer: email
mobile app event — app screen dwell. screen: bill summary; dwell seconds: 25
cloud event — marketing event. type: email opened; campaign: competitor price match offer
mobile app event — app open. screen: coverage map; dwell seconds: 18

verticals.label: Marketplace
verticals.description: Buyer WISMO risk vs delivery confidence — logistics outcomes

eval.methodology: Illustrative offline evaluation on 62 simulated held-out sessions with authored ground-truth labels. Included to show the shape of a model health ledger, not as a real benchmark.

smooth delivery experience with no issues

scenarios.label: Delivery delay session
scenarios.description: A carrier delay lands while the buyer is already refreshing the tracking page. Signals agree — WISMO contact is imminent, so the system moves before the ticket exists.

payload.screen: order tracking — where is my package

events.serialized: mobile app event — tracking page view. screen: order tracking — where is my package; action: asked where is my order; views today: 4; dwell seconds: 95

payload.screen: order tracking — refreshed again within the hour

payload.note: buyer rated delivery speed 2 of 5 stars

events.serialized: cloud event — crm event. type: delivery feedback from last order; note: buyer rated delivery speed 2 of 5 stars

scenarios.label: Anxious buyer, calm carrier
scenarios.description: The carrier's day-old scans say everything is on time; the buyer's live behavior says they're getting anxious anyway. Fast decay sides with the fresh anxiety — but automation is muted while sources disagree.

payload.screen: order tracking — checked again this morning

scenarios.label: Sparse / early order
scenarios.description: Label barely created, one casual app open. Nothing to infer from — the honest answer is the general queue, not a guessed intervention.

verticals.label: Fintech
verticals.description: Case escalation risk vs self-serve resolution — service routing

eval.methodology: Illustrative offline evaluation on 62 simulated held-out sessions with authored ground-truth labels. Included to show the shape of a model health ledger, not as a real benchmark.

no further contact needed after resolution steps

scenarios.label: Distress cluster
scenarios.description: A retry loop, a card freeze, and a chargeback land together. High-confidence escalation — route to a senior agent with the brief before the angry call happens.

events.serialized: mobile app event — transfer retry. action: retried failed transfer; attempts today: 3; amount usd: 1200
mobile app event — card action. action: froze card after disputed charge; screen: card security
cloud event — dispute webhook. type: chargeback filed; merchant: online electronics store; amount usd: 480
mobile app event — app screen dwell. screen: dispute status — case D-4432; views today: 4

payload.topic: asked why the transfer keeps failing

events.serialized: cloud event — support ticket. topic: asked why the transfer keeps failing; sentiment: frustrated; channel: chat

scenarios.label: History says patient, behavior says not
scenarios.description: The record says this customer resolves things amicably — refund accepted, 9/10 CSAT. Their live behavior says otherwise. Decay sides with today; automation holds while sources disagree.

events.serialized: mobile app event — transfer retry. action: retried failed transfer; attempts today: 2
cloud event — case history. type: previous dispute resolved amicably; outcome: refund accepted, positive feedback
mobile app event — card action. action: froze card after disputed charge

scenarios.label: Sparse / quiet case
scenarios.description: Case open, merchant hasn't responded, customer barely active. No basis to jump the queue in either direction — standard track.
